title: gmelina evoluta
intro: Gmelina evoluta, also known as the Forest Teak or Yellow Teak, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Asia. The bark and leaves of the Gmelina evoluta plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Gmelina evoluta include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves can be made into decoctions, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
